en.Brandenburg, Brandenburg, Deutschland: Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Germany

nameMeanings.name: Markgraf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Wolf.

German and Dutch: status name from Middle High German marcgrāve ‘margrave’ (which is a title derived from marc ‘boundary’ + grāve ‘royal judge’). In some cases the title may have been applied as a nickname for someone with overbearing behavior or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a margrave.

variants.name: Margraf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Mathias.
German and Dutch (of German origin): variant of   Markgraf  .

variants.name: Margrave
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Wolf.

German and Dutch: status name from Middle High German marcgrāve ‘margrave’ (which is a title derived from marc ‘boundary’ + grāve ‘royal judge’). In some cases the title may have been applied as a nickname for someone with overbearing behavior or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a margrave.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Brandenburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from Brandenburg, the name of a province, its principal city, and numerous other places.

variants.name: Brandenberg
meanings: German and Swiss German: habitational name from any of several minor places called Brandenberg, for example in Bavaria and Rhineland.
German: in some cases, probably also a variant of   Brandenburg  .

variants.name: Brandeberry
meanings: Americanized form of German   Brandenburg  .

variants.name: Brandenburger
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from Brandenburg (see   Brandenburg  ).

nameMeanings.name: Hermann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heri ‘army’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg was born in 1280, his father, Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg, was 34 and his mother, Judith von Henneberg-Coburg, was 33. He married Anna Herzogin von Österreich in 1295. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. He died on 1 January 1308, at the age of 28, and was buried in Lehnin, Zauch-Belzig, Brandenburg, Prussia.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg est né en 1280, son père, Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Judith von Henneberg-Coburg, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Anna Herzogin von Österreich en 1295. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1308, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterré à Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Kurfürstentum Brandenburg, Prusse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg 1280 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg 34 und seine Mutter Judith von Henneberg-Coburg 33. Er hat 1295 Anna Herzogin von Österreich geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 1. Januar 1308 im Alter von 28 Jahren und wurde in Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Kurfürstentum Brandenburg, Preußen begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg è nato il nel 1280, il suo padre, Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Judith von Henneberg-Coburg, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Anna Herzogin von Österreich nel 1295. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1308, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolto a Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Kurfürstentum Brandenburg, Preußen.

ko.lifeSummary: Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg(이)가 1280년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith von Henneberg-Coburg(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1295년 Anna Herzogin von Österreich(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1308년 1월 1일 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 프레우ss 커프u르스투 브랜덴버그 크레이스 자ch-벨지그 레h닌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg nasceu em 1280, seu pai, Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Judith von Henneberg-Coburg, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Herzogin von Österreich em 1295. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hermann II. Markgraf von Brandenburg родился в 1280 г., его отцу по имени Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Judith von Henneberg-Coburg, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Herzogin von Österreich в 1295 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Он умер 1 января 1308 г. в возрасте 28 лет и был похоронен в местности лэнин, крис зауч-белзиг, курфuрстентум брэнденбург, Пруссия.
